summary: All previously-blocking items are now addressed, including the remaining multi-tenant storage isolation requirement, and no new truly blocking issues were introduced.

previous_issues_status.original_issue: Fargate Spot cost estimate was off by an order of magnitude; needed hours-based, region-specific math and explicit variability/fallback
previous_issues_status.notes: NFR-1 includes correct us-east-1 vCPU-hr/GB-hr math over 730 hours and explicitly calls out Spot discount variability plus a Cost Explorer validation step.

previous_issues_status.original_issue: SQLite on EFS was underspecified/risky; needed explicit PRAGMAs, mount/access constraints, and operational constraints for interruptions/deploys
previous_issues_status.notes: FR-1 now specifies WAL/NORMAL/busy_timeout, acknowledges EFS fsync latency, adds a performance gate, and constrains the workload to low-traffic with explicit stop-then-start semantics.

previous_issues_status.original_issue: Missing ECS service deployment settings for a stateful singleton (avoid two tasks overlapping on same SQLite file)
previous_issues_status.notes: FR-1 sets deployment min healthy = 0 and max = 100 with a grace period, which is the key control to prevent overlapping tasks during deployments.

previous_issues_status.original_issue: ALB listener rule priority collisions not addressed; needed deterministic allocation/collision handling
previous_issues_status.notes: FR-2 includes a deterministic hash-based priority scheme, a reserved range, and plan-time collision validation, which resolves the apply-failure risk.

previous_issues_status.original_issue: Multi-tenant isolation: POSIX UID/GID alone is insufficient; needed EFS IAM authorization + access point enforcement + task role scoping
previous_issues_status.notes: NFR-2 now clearly states the primary boundary is EFS IAM authorization + access point enforcement with task-role permissions scoped to the access point ARN(s), and explicitly demotes POSIX UID/GID to defense-in-depth. It also adds an explicit external-builder gate requiring per-world SGs before onboarding, removing the prior ambiguity.

previous_issues_status.original_issue: Finn gateway auth model underspecified (token lifecycle/claims/tenant scoping) and internal TLS posture unclear
previous_issues_status.notes: FR-3 defines token storage/rotation, required claims including world_id/env/aud/exp, JWKS validation, and explicitly documents HTTP-in-VPC as an accepted risk with a future mTLS note.

previous_issues_status.original_issue: GitHub Actions OIDC trust policy constraints missing; risk of role assumption from wrong repo/branch
previous_issues_status.notes: FR-4 requires OIDC-only, exact repo+branch subject restriction, aud=sts.amazonaws.com, and least-privilege scoping to the world’s ECR/ECS resources.
